agents.name: Rex
agents.note:
US-355 LANDED (I-040 structural close = deploy-context drive simulator harness). Sprint 41 / V0.27.17 COMPLETE -- all 7 stories US-350..US-356 passes:true. Pytest harness at tests/integration/test_deploy_context_drive_simulator.py exercises integrated Pi-sqlite -> sync -> server-compute path against real DBs with no mock of writer/compute seams. ONE scenario seeded per Atlas Q5 minimum-viable: V0.27.16 drive-20 reproducer (drive_id=20, 540s @ 1Hz, 4-PID subset of Argus's 16, sequencer-terminated with zero drive_end / engine_off connection_log events). 6 tests / 2 classes: TestScenario1V0_27_16Reproducer ships GREEN (B-104 compute invoked -> drive_summary computed fields non-NULL + arithmetically consistent + drive_statistics 4 rows with full data_quality) + RED (no compute -- faithfully mirrors V0.27.16 architecture where Pi-side recorder waited on never-fired engine_off + server _tryAutoAnalysisTrigger required preconditions never materialized -> drive_summary fields NULL + drive_statistics zero rows = exact pattern Argus drilled on drive 20). TestHarnessIntegrity (4): pins 'no mock seams' + 'real DBs' + 'compute imports are production' (via identity check on the compute callables) + 'scenario does not fire drive-end' (zero connection_log drive_end/engine_off events post-scenario-build) so a future weakening trips RED here. Retroactive RED proof per Atlas's accepted option ladder: Option A (in-tree architectural-cut parameterization) shipped as the RED test; Option B (out-of-tree git-worktree against V0.27.16 ship anchor c04d36e) documented with reproducible commands in the spec doc; Option C (--baseline-commit pytest plugin) deferred V0.28+. Spec doc docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-21-deploy-context-drive-simulator.md ships at 1.5 pages: harness design (component-layer table mapping production source to harness fixture), 'what is NOT mocked' explicit list, scenario 1 reproducer table mirroring Argus drill, retroactive RED proof option ladder with full B procedure (git worktree commands against c04d36e), sign-off chain table for Atlas+Argus+Ralph+Marcus, 6-scenario roadmap for V0.28+ (sparse / replay / partial-sync / idempotent re-run / multi-drive batch / Spool Drive 11+15+18 reference signatures per Spool FLAG-5), 4 harness invariants, cross-links. Verification gates GREEN: pytest tests/integration/test_deploy_context_drive_simulator.py -v 6/6 in 41s; pytest tests/integration/ -v 16 passed in 49s (10 pre-existing test_pi_to_server_e2e.py + test_pi_to_server_sync_recovery.py untouched = zero regressions); pytest tests/server/analytics/ + tests/pi/obdii/test_drive_statistics_pi_table_migration.py 31 passed in 59s (US-350 + US-351 + Pi retirement suites all clean); ruff All checks passed on the new test file. Sign-off status: Ralph axis CLOSED; Marcus axis CLOSED on sprint.json land; Atlas + Argus axes pending independent reviews (per US-355 bigDoD #6 + validation.bigDefinitionOfDone US-355 entry). Scope-fence honored: only the 2 in-story NEW files touched (zero production code changes; regression_manifest.json untouched -- Tester's lane). Sprint 41 status after US-355: 7-of-7 stories LANDED (US-350+US-351+US-352+US-353+US-354+US-355+US-356 all passes:true) -- emitting <promise>COMPLETE</promise>; PRD COMPLETE per ralph.sh tag parsing rules.

[Prior session note for context] US-354 LANDED (deploy-pi.sh daemon-reload + service restart gap). Atlas Finding 1 condition-gating fix per the 2026-05-21 accuracy review: step_install_power_watch_unit's `sudo systemctl restart eclipse-powerwatch.service` is now DECOUPLED from the `$changed` (unit-file-diff) gate -- restart fires unconditionally on every default deploy because Python source updates ride rsync and need process replacement even when the unit file is byte-identical (the V0.27.16 dead-code-in-memory bug Argus caught 2026-05-21: Pi ran V0.27.15 code in memory for 15h despite .deploy-version reporting V0.27.16). daemon-reload above stays gated on $changed -- unit-file metadata reload is no-op when files are identical; the two concerns now correctly separated. NEW DEPLOY_START_EPOCH=$(date +%s) captured right after flag-parsing, before any step runs (so any restart fired during the deploy yields a service-start later than this value). NEW step_verify_service_restarts() between step_restart_service and step_write_deploy_version: ssh-side verify_one_service() resolves ExecMainStartTimestamp via systemctl show -p, converts to epoch via date -d, compares against DEPLOY_START_EPOCH; per-service WARN if not installed (fresh-Pi case), FAIL+exit 9 if start time precedes deploy-start. Both eclipse-powerwatch + eclipse-obd checked so a future regression to the same bug class on eclipse-obd is caught structurally. Bottom dispatch REORDERED from (write-version -> restart-service) to (restart-service -> verify -> write-version) so a failed verification leaves .deploy-version at the prior release. Pattern-class lesson: condition-gating defect (narrow predicate $changed silently absorbing a broader case 'code changed') is the same tokenization as F-7's edge-only post-grace check (Sprint 40); the fix decouples the two predicates rather than re-narrowing. 10 new static-content tests in tests/deploy/test_deploy_pi.py with 2 new helpers (_scriptText, _stepBody) -- _stepBody scopes assertions to one function body so a global grep cannot confuse restart-inside-step_install_power_watch_unit with restart-inside-step_restart_service. Verification gates GREEN: pytest tests/deploy/test_deploy_pi.py 15/15 (5 pre-existing + 10 new); pytest tests/deploy/ 153/153 (baseline 141 + 12 new = 10 static + 2 wrapping smoke); bash tests/deploy/test_deploy_pi.sh 29/29 (--dry-run preserved offline-safe); ruff All checks passed on the touched test file. deploy-server.sh UNTOUCHED per doNotTouch invariant. Sprint 41 status after US-354: 6-of-7 stories LANDED (US-350, US-351, US-352, US-353, US-354, US-356 all passes:true); 1 story remains pending (US-355 deploy-context drive simulator harness, M-size, depends on US-350/US-351 already done); sprint not complete; emitting no <promise> tag so ralph.sh starts next iteration.

[Prior session note for context] US-353 LANDED (US-345 maxTrailBytes guard fix; Atlas-preferred auto-trim shape). src/pi/diagnostics/boot_progress.py markMilestone refactored: refuse-to-write block replaced with new private _autoTrimAndAppend helper that drops complete oldest lines line-boundary-preserving until the new line fits under maxTrailBytes, WARN-logs the trim event with was/trimmed_to/stage/maxTrailBytes tokens, rewrites via tmp + os.replace for atomicity. Contract: every rung ALWAYS lands on disk (closes the observability hole Argus's V0.27.16 drill found where a trail accumulated from F-8-broken regime caused the first post-deploy reboot to drop POWEROFF_INVOKED + every subsequent rung). deriveVerdict MAX(rank) semantic preserved because trimming oldest can only LOSE early-rung signal, never CORRUPT the verdict -- arm() truncates between boots anyway. 5 new tests added + 1 renamed in tests/pi/diagnostics/test_boot_progress.py: autoTrimsAtMaxTrailBytes (renamed from stopsAtMaxTrailBytes; assertion strengthened to confirm MOST RECENT rung is on disk), dropsOldestNotNewest (POWEROFF_INVOKED survives trim of WARNING-rung seed), warnsWithTrimmedSize (caplog asserts was/maxTrailBytes tokens), neverRefusesWrite (pre-seed past cap then write DRAIN_CLOSED -- contract test), threeConsecutiveCleanReboots_doNotTripTrim (full 9-rung ladder x 3 cycles with arm() between -- zero trim WARNs as bigDoD requires for post-F-8-fix flow), forcedLargeTrail_autoTrimsAndWarns (pre-seed 95% of cap + 10 writes trip trim + WARN + bound + new rung on disk; Atlas Refinement C 30-min cap honored at ~10 min build cost). F-8 fix preserved (deploy/boot-progress-finalize.service unchanged); CLEAN_COMPLETE emission identical to Sprint 40 ship; startup_log schema untouched. Verification gates GREEN: pytest tests/pi/diagnostics/test_boot_progress.py 23/23; pytest tests/pi/diagnostics/ 53/53 (zero regressions); ruff All checks passed on both touched files. IRL acceptance gate (bigDoD US-353): post-V0.27.17 deploy + first reboot does NOT trip the guard. Sprint 41 status after US-353: 5-of-7 stories LANDED (US-350, US-351, US-352, US-353, US-356 all passes:true); 2 stories remain pending (US-354 deploy-pi.sh daemon-reload+restart gap, US-355 deploy-context drive simulator harness); sprint not complete; emitting no <promise> tag so ralph.sh starts next iteration.

[Prior session note for context] US-356 LANDED (PM Rule 10 design-gate DoD for Sprint 41 / V0.27.17). Specs-only story: new Section 10.7 'Data Pipeline Architecture (B-104 Step 1, Sprint 41 / V0.27.17)' appended to specs/architecture.md between section 10.6 Shutdown Sequencer (Sprint 40 ratification preserved untouched) and section 11 Deployment Architecture, paralleling the Â§10.6 structural shape per Atlas Q6 verdict. 258-line addition across 8 subsections covering all 6 required content axes (a-f) from acceptance: architectural principle (Pi = telemetry emitter; server = sole authority), compute path (drive_summary_compute + drive_statistics_compute modules with Atlas Q2 NULL-preservation + Spool FLAG-1 SSOT pin + Atlas Refinements A/B), Pi-side retirement scope (drive_statistics table + module + wiring entirely retired via ensureDriveStatisticsRetired idempotent DROP), trigger seam shift (nightly batch + on-demand CLI replace deleted _tryAutoAnalysisTrigger seam; NotImplementedError tripwire on enqueueAutoAnalysisForSync re-introduction), idempotent recompute principle (DELETE-then-INSERT + computed_at onupdate), what's retired (4-row table cross-linking US-326/US-328/US-348/US-349 to anchor SHAs 76aa773 + 0599d24 + 1c01ec0 + c04d36e + b26344e + 5fb7cdc + e6c49e6 = 7 SHAs total, >=4 required). 'Until that drill clears, this section describes the deployed architecture intent, not the validated production state.' Gate-ratification subsection cites the 2026-05-18 design-gate governance rule + Atlas's 2026-05-21 per-task gate pre-registration note + the SSOT-pattern-load-bearing observation note.
